turnaround_reason: Stock plunged ~65% from its 52-week high of $410 after disappointing FY2026 guidance ($6.5B revenue vs $6.7B consensus; EPS $12.30 vs $13.53 consensus) despite beating Q4 EPS estimates. The sell-off appears overdone for a high-margin subscription business with 69% gross margins and $1B+ FCF, but revenue growth is decelerating (-1.5% TTM) and debt/equity is extreme at 5,293%.
bullish_points: Forward PE of 9.4x is historically cheap for Gartner — market is pricing permanent decline
4 consecutive quarters of EPS beats averaging ~12% surprise — execution is intact
EV/EBITDA of 8.4x is well below 5-year average ~15x for this quality of franchise
Strong FCF of $1.06B (FCF yield ~11% at current price) supports debt paydown
Analyst mean target of $166.50 implies 16% upside from current ~$144
bearish_points: Revenue growth turned negative (-1.5% TTM) — Gartner's subscription model is losing volume
Extreme leverage: debt/equity 5,293%, current ratio 0.94 — balance sheet is fragile
25% short interest signals significant smart money betting against recovery
Management guided well below consensus, suggesting they see continued headwinds
Price/book of 154x means any further earnings disappointment devastates the stock
catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings (Aug 4, 2026) — if management raises guidance or shows revenue re-acceleration, the stock could snap back sharply given the compressed multiple
suggested_trade: Pass or use a small put-spread to capture asymmetric recovery — the debt load and negative growth make this speculative despite cheap multiples
key_risk: Revenue decline proves structural (enterprises cutting research spend permanently), and leverage forces dividend cuts or dilution
would_skip_if: Q2 earnings show further revenue deceleration or if leverage metrics deteriorate further
